title: Shattering Indulgent Korean Lamb Shoulder Bulgogi
description: A rich and savory Korean-inspired lamb shoulder dish, featuring a sweet and spicy sauce made with dashi extract, that is easy to make and perfect for a special occasion.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together dashi extract, soy sauce, gochujang, brown sugar, black pepper, and Korean chili flakes.
Add the lamb shoulder to the bowl and coat with the marinade, refrigerating overnight or for at least 4 hours.
Heat the coconut oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the lamb shoulder until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Transfer the lamb to a plate and set aside.
Add more coconut oil to the pot if needed, then sauté the ginger and garlic until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Add the green onions and toasted sesame seeds to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Return the lamb shoulder to the pot and cover with a lid, transferring to the preheated oven at 325°F (165°C).
Braise the lamb for 2-3 hours, or until it is tender and easily shreds with a fork.
Strain the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve into a small saucepan, discarding the solids.
Bring the sauce to a simmer over medium heat and cook until it has thickened, about 5-7 minutes.
Shred the lamb with two forks and toss with the sauce, serving hot.
Garnish with toasted sesame seeds and green onions, if desired.
Refrigerate leftovers for up to 3 days and reheat as needed.
Freeze for up to 2 months, thawing overnight in the refrigerator before reheating.
